heading: 9.7.16.3.Major-ness supported by Strides
content:
There are two strides involved while accessing a matrix from shared memory:

1. Leading dimension stride (byte offset or absolute address)
2. Stride dimension byte offset

##### 9.7.16.3.1. [Leading Dimension Stride: relative offset or absolute address](#tcgen05-leading-dimension-byte-offset)

There are two modes of Leading Dimension Strides as described below.
Bit #52 in the [Shared memory descriptor](#tcgen05-shared-memory-descriptor) is used to distinguish between two modes.

###### 9.7.16.3.1.1. [Relative offset mode](#tcgen05-leading-dimension-byte-offset-relative-offset)

In this mode, the leading dimension stride is specified as a relative byte offset between the
columns as explained in the below table.
The leading dimension stride can either be specified as a relative offset between the columns
or as an absolute byte address of next buffer. The leading dimension stride is defined
differently for transposed and non-transposed matrices. The leading dimension stride is defined
as follows for matrices whose element types are normalized to 128-bits:

| Major-ness | Definition |
| --- | --- |
| K-Major | * No-Swizzling: the stride from the first column to the second column   of the 8x2 tile in the 128-bit element type normalized matrix. * Swizzled layouts: not used, assumed to be 1. |
| MN-Major | * Interleave: stride from the first 8 columns to the next 8 columns. * Swizzled layouts: stride from the first (swizzle-byte-size/16) rows   to the next (swizzle-byte-size/16) rows. |

###### 9.7.16.3.1.2. [Absolute address mode for K dimension being 48B](#tcgen05-leading-dimension-byte-offset-absolute-address)

The `tcgen05.mma` instruction with *K-dimension* of 48B would overflow the 128B
shared memory boundary if the data is packed contiguously.

In this case, the absolute address mode can be used to break up the data in the
shared memory into two chunks such that both these chunks are laid out within
the aligned 128-byte address boundary.
The leading dimension absolute address can point to the second data chunk in the shared memory.

###### 9.7.16.3.1.2.1. [Restrictions on the Leading Dimension Absolute Address Stride](#tcgen05-leading-dimension-byte-offset-absolute-address-restriction)

Following are the restrictions on the absolute address stride mode:

1. Only 128B swizzle (with 16B atomicity) is supported.
2. Only K-Major mode is supported. That is, the transpose bits(bits #15 and #16) in
   [Instruction descriptor](#tcgen05-instruction-descriptor) must be 0.
3. The matrix base offset must be 0.

##### 9.7.16.3.2. [Stride Dimension Byte Offset](#tcgen05-stride-dimension-byte-offset)

The stride dimension byte offset is defined differently for transposed and non-transposed
matrices. The stride dimension byte offset is defined as follows for matrices whose element
types are normalized to 128-bits:

| Major-ness | Definition |
| --- | --- |
| K-Major | The offset from the first 8 rows to the next 8 rows. |
| MN-Major | * Interleave: offset from the first row to the next row. * Swizzled layout: offset from the first 8 columns to the next 8   columns |

##### 9.7.16.3.3. [Canonical Layouts](#tcgen05-canonical-layouts)

In terms of [CuTe layouts](https://docs.nvidia.com/cutlass/media/docs/cpp/cute/01_layout.html)
the canonical layout can be expressed as follows:

| Major- ness | Swizzling mode | Canonical Layout without swizzling | [Swizzling](https://github.com/NVIDIA/cutlass/blob/bf9da7b76c766d7ee7d536afc77880a4ef1f1156/include/cute/swizzle.hpp) on the previous column |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| MN- major | No-swizzling or Interleaved | ((T,1,m),(8,k)):((1,T,SBO),(1T,LBO)) | Swizzle<0, 4, 3> |
| 32B Swizzling | ((T,2,m),(8,k)):((1,T,LBO),(2T,SBO)) | Swizzle<1, 4, 3> |
| 64B Swizzling | ((T,4,m),(8,k)):((1,T,LBO),(4T,SBO)) | Swizzle<2, 4, 3> |
| 128B Swizzling | ((T,8,m),(8,k)):((1,T,LBO),(8T,SBO)) | Swizzle<3, 4, 3> |
| K- major | No-swizzling or Interleaved | ((8,m),(T,2k)):((1T,SBO),(1,LBO)) | Swizzle<0, 4, 3> |
| 32B Swizzling | ((8,m),(T,2k)):((2T,SBO),(1,T)) | Swizzle<1, 4, 3> |
| 64B Swizzling | ((8,m),(T,2k)):((4T,SBO),(1,T)) | Swizzle<2, 4, 3> |
| 128B Swizzling | ((8,m),(T,2k)):((8T,SBO),(1,T)) | Swizzle<3, 4, 3> |

where

* T = 128 / sizeof-elements-in-bits
  T represents scale factor which normalizes matrix element types to 128-bits.
* m represents the number of repeating patterns across rows.
* k represents the number of repeating patterns across columns.

Examples

* K-Major, no-swizzling and tf32 type: [Figure 188](#tcgen05-k-no-swizzle-tf32)

  

[Figure: async-warpgroup-k-no-swizzle-tf32]
CONCEPT BEING ILLUSTRATED:
This figure illustrates the memory layout mapping for a shared memory matrix tile using K-major ordering with no swizzling and tf32 (tensor float 32) data type. It shows how 256 elements (numbered 0 through 255) are arranged in a 16x16 grid, indicating which thread or memory location accesses which element index. This is relevant to the ldmatrix or wgmma (warp group matrix multiply-accumulate) instructions in PTX ISA where shared memory layout determines data access patterns.

GRID DIMENSIONS:
The grid is 16 columns wide (labeled 0 through 15 along the top) and 16 rows tall (labeled 0 through 15 along the left side). This forms a 16x16 = 256 element matrix.

ELEMENT NUMBERING PATTERN:
Each cell contains a unique integer from 0 to 255. The numbering does NOT follow a simple row-major sequential order across the full grid. Instead, the pattern reflects K-major (column-major in the K dimension) ordering with a specific tiling structure.

The values in each row are as follows:

Row 0:  0,   1,   2,   3,   64,  65,  66,  67,  128, 129, 130, 131, 192, 193, 194, 195
Row 1:  4,   5,   6,   7,   68,  69,  70,  71,  132, 133, 134, 135, 196, 197, 198, 199
Row 2:  8,   9,   10,  11,  72,  73,  74,  75,  136, 137, 138, 139, 200, 201, 202, 203
Row 3:  12,  13,  14,  15,  76,  77,  78,  79,  140, 141, 142, 143, 204, 205, 206, 207
Row 4:  16,  17,  18,  19,  80,  81,  82,  83,  144, 145, 146, 147, 208, 209, 210, 211
Row 5:  20,  21,  22,  23,  84,  85,  86,  87,  148, 149, 150, 151, 212, 213, 214, 215
Row 6:  24,  25,  26,  27,  88,  89,  90,  91,  152, 153, 154, 155, 216, 217, 218, 219
Row 7:  28,  29,  30,  31,  92,  93,  94,  95,  156, 157, 158, 159, 220, 221, 222, 223
Row 8:  32,  33,  34,  35,  96,  97,  98,  99,  160, 161, 162, 163, 224, 225, 226, 227
Row 9:  36,  37,  38,  39,  100, 101, 102, 103, 164, 165, 166, 167, 228, 229, 230, 231
Row 10: 40,  41,  42,  43,  104, 105, 106, 107, 168, 169, 170, 171, 232, 233, 234, 235
Row 11: 44,  45,  46,  47,  108, 109, 110, 111, 172, 173, 174, 175, 236, 237, 238, 239
Row 12: 48,  49,  50,  51,  112, 113, 114, 115, 176, 177, 178, 179, 240, 241, 242, 243
Row 13: 52,  53,  54,  55,  116, 117, 118, 119, 180, 181, 182, 183, 244, 245, 246, 247
Row 14: 56,  57,  58,  59,  120, 121, 122, 123, 184, 185, 186, 187, 248, 249, 250, 251
Row 15: 60,  61,  62,  63,  124, 125, 126, 127, 188, 189, 190, 191, 252, 253, 254, 255

TILING STRUCTURE AND PATTERN ANALYSIS:
The 16x16 grid is divided into four horizontal bands of 4 columns each (columns 0-3, columns 4-7, columns 8-11, columns 12-15). Within each band:
- Columns 0-3: elements 0-63, sequential in groups of 4 per row, incrementing by 4 per row downward (0,1,2,3 in row 0; 4,5,6,7 in row 1; ... 60,61,62,63 in row 15)
- Columns 4-7: elements 64-127, same pattern offset by 64
- Columns 8-11: elements 128-191, same pattern offset by 128
- Columns 12-15: elements 192-255, same pattern offset by 192

Within each 4-column band, elements increment by 1 across the 4 columns and by 4 going down each row. This means the K-major layout stores consecutive K-dimension elements contiguously in groups of 4, then proceeds down the row (M) dimension.

COLOR CODING:
The rows are color-coded in a repeating 4-row cycle to visually group sets of 4 rows together:
- Rows 0, 4, 8, 12: Blue/purple background
- Rows 1, 5, 9, 13: Green background
- Rows 2, 6, 10, 14: Yellow/light yellow background
- Rows 3, 7, 11, 15: Pink/salmon/red background

This 4-row color grouping repeats four times across the 16 rows, likely indicating warp-level or thread-group-level data partitioning.

AXIS LABELS:
- Top axis (columns): labeled 0 through 15 from left to right
- Left axis (rows): labeled 0 through 15 from top to bottom
- No additional axis titles are shown in the figure itself; the caption provides the context: K major, no-swizzling, tf32 type

SUMMARY OF DATA ORGANIZATION:
The layout divides the 16x16 tile into four 16x4 vertical slabs. Each slab holds 64 consecutive elements. Within each slab, elements are stored in row-major order with 4 elements per row. The K-major designation means the K (inner/reduction) dimension varies fastest within each group of 4 consecutive elements. The no-swizzling designation means there is no XOR-based address permutation applied to avoid bank conflicts. The tf32 type means each element is a 32-bit tensor float value, so each element occupies 4 bytes in memory.


  Figure 188 K major, no-swizzling and tf32 type

  the strides and related details are as follows:

  Exact layout : Swizzle<0,4,3> o ((8,2),(4,4)):((4,32),(1,64))

  Canonical Layout :Swizzle<0,4,3> o ((8,m),(T,2k)):((1T,SBO),(1,LBO))

  | Parameters | Value |
  | --- | --- |
  | T | 4 |
  | m | 2 |
  | k | 2 |
  | LBO (relative offset) | 64\*sizeof(tf32) |
  | SBO | 32\*sizeof(tf32) |
  | Encoding of LBO in descriptor | (LBO) >> 4 = 16 |
  | Encoding of SBO in descriptor | (SBO) >> 4 = 8 |
* K-Major, 32B swizzling and tf32 type: [Figure 189](#tcgen05-k-32b-swizzle-tf32)

  

[Figure: async-warpgroup-k-32B-swizzle-tf32]
This figure illustrates the memory layout and swizzling pattern for a K-major matrix with 32-byte swizzling and tf32 data type, as used in NVIDIA PTX ISA wgmma (warp group matrix multiply-accumulate) or similar tensor core operations.

OVERALL STRUCTURE:
The figure is a 16x16 grid of cells. The rows are labeled 0 through 15 on the left side (vertical axis). The columns are labeled 0 through 15 on the top (horizontal axis). Each cell contains a numerical value representing a memory element index (i.e., the logical-to-physical address mapping after swizzling is applied).

COLOR CODING (indicating swizzle groups or bank assignments):
The cells are colored in a repeating pattern of four colors across groups of rows, cycling every 4 rows:
- Purple/blue: rows 0, 4, 8, 12 (and specific column groupings within)
- Green: rows 1, 5, 9, 13
- Yellow: rows 2, 6, 10, 14
- Pink/red: rows 3, 7, 11, 15

Within each row, the coloring also shifts based on column groupings, reflecting the swizzle pattern. Specifically, within each row the color of cells shifts in groups of 4 columns, creating a diagonal swizzle pattern across the grid.

NUMERICAL VALUES IN EACH CELL (row by row, columns 0-15):

Row 0:  0,   1,   2,   3,   4,   5,   6,   7,  128, 129, 130, 131, 132, 133, 134, 135
Row 1:  8,   9,  10,  11,  12,  13,  14,  15,  136, 137, 138, 139, 140, 141, 142, 143
Row 2: 16,  17,  18,  19,  20,  21,  22,  23,  144, 145, 146, 147, 148, 149, 150, 151
Row 3: 24,  25,  26,  27,  28,  29,  30,  31,  152, 153, 154, 155, 156, 157, 158, 159
Row 4: 36,  37,  38,  39,  32,  33,  34,  35,  164, 165, 166, 167, 160, 161, 162, 163
Row 5: 44,  45,  46,  47,  40,  41,  42,  43,  172, 173, 174, 175, 168, 169, 170, 171
Row 6: 52,  53,  54,  55,  48,  49,  50,  51,  180, 181, 182, 183, 176, 177, 178, 179
Row 7: 60,  61,  62,  63,  56,  57,  58,  59,  188, 189, 190, 191, 184, 185, 186, 187
Row 8: 64,  65,  66,  67,  68,  69,  70,  71,  192, 193, 194, 195, 196, 197, 198, 199
Row 9: 72,  73,  74,  75,  76,  77,  78,  79,  200, 201, 202, 203, 204, 205, 206, 207
Row 10: 80,  81,  82,  83,  84,  85,  86,  87,  208, 209, 210, 211, 212, 213, 214, 215
Row 11: 88,  89,  90,  91,  92,  93,  94,  95,  216, 217, 218, 219, 220, 221, 222, 223
Row 12: 100, 101, 102, 103,  96,  97,  98,  99,  228, 229, 230, 231, 224, 225, 226, 227
Row 13: 108, 109, 110, 111, 104, 105, 106, 107,  236, 237, 238, 239, 232, 233, 234, 235
Row 14: 116, 117, 118, 119, 112, 113, 114, 115,  244, 245, 246, 247, 240, 241, 242, 243
Row 15: 124, 125, 126, 127, 120, 121, 122, 123,  252, 253, 254, 255, 248, 249, 250, 251

SWIZZLE PATTERN ANALYSIS:
The tf32 type is 4 bytes per element. With 32-byte swizzling, groups of 8 tf32 elements (8 x 4 = 32 bytes) form a swizzle unit.

The column axis (0-15) represents the K dimension (K-major layout). The row axis (0-15) represents the other matrix dimension.

Key observations about the swizzle:
- Rows 0-3 (first group of 4): columns 0-7 hold indices 0-31 sequentially (no swap), columns 8-15 hold indices 128-159 sequentially.
- Rows 4-7 (second group of 4): columns 0-3 and 4-7 are swapped relative to sequential order (e.g., row 4 has 36,37,38,39 in cols 0-3 and 32,33,34,35 in cols 4-7), and similarly for the right half.
- Rows 8-11 (third group of 4): same pattern as rows 0-3 (no swap within the half-row groups).
- Rows 12-15 (fourth group of 4): same swap pattern as rows 4-7.

The swizzle effectively XORs the column group index with a function of the row index to avoid shared memory bank conflicts when loading tf32 data in K-major order. The left half (columns 0-7) contains indices in the range 0-127 and the right half (columns 8-15) contains indices in the range 128-255, with the swizzle applied within each half independently.


  Figure 189 K major, 32B swizzling and tf32 type

  the strides and related details are as follows:

  Exact layout : Swizzle<1,4,3> o ((8,2),(4,4)):((8,64),(1,4))

  Canonical Layout :Swizzle<1,4,3> o ((8,m),(T,2k)):((2T,SBO),(1,T))

  | Parameters | Value |
  | --- | --- |
  | T | 4 |
  | m | 2 |
  | k | 2 |
  | LBO (relative offset) | NA |
  | SBO | 64\*sizeof(tf32) |
  | Encoding of LBO in descriptor | 1 (assumed) |
  | Encoding of SBO in descriptor | (SBO) >> 4 = 16 |
* MN-Major, no-swizzling and bf16 type: [Figure 190](#tcgen05-mn-no-swizzle-bf16)

  

[Figure: async-warpgroup-mn-no-swizzle-bf16]
CONCEPT BEING ILLUSTRATED:
This figure illustrates the memory layout mapping for a tensor memory access pattern in NVIDIA PTX ISA, specifically for the MN-major ordering with no swizzling and bf16 (bfloat16) data type. It shows how element indices are arranged in a 16x16 grid, mapping logical matrix positions (row, column) to memory offset values.

GRID STRUCTURE:
The figure is a 16-row by 16-column grid (rows 0-15, columns 0-15). The column indices (0 through 15) are labeled across the top. The row indices (0 through 15) are labeled down the left side.

COLOR CODING (row-based pattern, repeating every 4 rows):
Row 0: Blue/purple background
Row 1: Green background
Row 2: Yellow/light yellow background
Row 3: Red/salmon/pink background
Row 4: Blue/purple background
Row 5: Green background
Row 6: Yellow/light yellow background
Row 7: Red/salmon/pink background
Row 8: Blue/purple background
Row 9: Green background
Row 10: Yellow/light yellow background
Row 11: Red/salmon/pink background
Row 12: Blue/purple background
Row 13: Green background
Row 14: Yellow/light yellow background
Row 15: Red/salmon/pink background

The color pattern repeats every 4 rows: blue, green, yellow, red.

NUMERICAL VALUES IN THE GRID (row, column -> value):

Row 0:  0, 8, 16, 24, 32, 40, 48, 56, 128, 136, 144, 152, 160, 168, 176, 184
Row 1:  1, 9, 17, 25, 33, 41, 49, 57, 129, 137, 145, 153, 161, 169, 177, 185
Row 2:  2, 10, 18, 26, 34, 42, 50, 58, 130, 138, 146, 154, 162, 170, 178, 186
Row 3:  3, 11, 19, 27, 35, 43, 51, 59, 131, 139, 147, 155, 163, 171, 179, 187
Row 4:  4, 12, 20, 28, 36, 44, 52, 60, 132, 140, 148, 156, 164, 172, 180, 188
Row 5:  5, 13, 21, 29, 37, 45, 53, 61, 133, 141, 149, 157, 165, 173, 181, 189
Row 6:  6, 14, 22, 30, 38, 46, 54, 62, 134, 142, 150, 158, 166, 174, 182, 190
Row 7:  7, 15, 23, 31, 39, 47, 55, 63, 135, 143, 151, 159, 167, 175, 183, 191
Row 8:  64, 72, 80, 88, 96, 104, 112, 120, 192, 200, 208, 216, 224, 232, 240, 248
Row 9:  65, 73, 81, 89, 97, 105, 113, 121, 193, 201, 209, 217, 225, 233, 241, 249
Row 10: 66, 74, 82, 90, 98, 106, 114, 122, 194, 202, 210, 218, 226, 234, 242, 250
Row 11: 67, 75, 83, 91, 99, 107, 115, 123, 195, 203, 211, 219, 227, 235, 243, 251
Row 12: 68, 76, 84, 92, 100, 108, 116, 124, 196, 204, 212, 220, 228, 236, 244, 252
Row 13: 69, 77, 85, 93, 101, 109, 117, 125, 197, 205, 213, 221, 229, 237, 245, 253
Row 14: 70, 78, 86, 94, 102, 110, 118, 126, 198, 206, 214, 222, 230, 238, 246, 254
Row 15: 71, 79, 87, 95, 103, 111, 119, 127, 199, 207, 215, 223, 231, 239, 247, 255

DATA ORGANIZATION PATTERNS:

1. The grid is divided into four 8x8 quadrants:
   - Top-left quadrant (rows 0-7, cols 0-7): values 0-63
   - Top-right quadrant (rows 0-7, cols 8-15): values 128-191
   - Bottom-left quadrant (rows 8-15, cols 0-7): values 64-127
   - Bottom-right quadrant (rows 8-15, cols 8-15): values 192-255

2. Within each quadrant, values increase by 1 going down each column (column-major / MN-major ordering). For example, in the top-left quadrant, column 0 contains 0,1,2,3,4,5,6,7 (rows 0-7).

3. Within each quadrant, values increase by 8 going across each row. For example, in the top-left quadrant, row 0 contains 0,8,16,24,32,40,48,56.

4. The stride between columns within a quadrant is 8 (one full column of 8 elements).

5. The quadrant ordering in memory is: top-left (0-63), bottom-left (64-127), top-right (128-191), bottom-right (192-255). This reflects MN-major layout where the M dimension (rows) is traversed first within each block, then N dimension blocks are laid out.

6. Total number of elements: 256 (values 0 through 255), consistent with a 16x16 matrix of bf16 elements.

SUMMARY OF LAYOUT FORMULA:
For a given cell at (row, col):
- The value represents the memory index/offset of that element.
- Column group: cols 0-7 are in the left half, cols 8-15 are in the right half.
- Row group: rows 0-7 are in the top half, rows 8-15 are in the bottom half.
- Within each 8x8 block, the index = block_base + (col_within_block * 8) + row_within_block.
- Block bases: top-left=0, bottom-left=64, top-right=128, bottom-right=192.


  Figure 190 MN major, no-swizzling and bf16 type

  the strides and related details are as follows:

  Exact layout : Swizzle<0,4,3> o ((8,1,2),(8,2)):((1,8,64),(8,128))

  Canonical Layout :Swizzle<0,4,3> o ((T,1,m),(8,k)):((1,T,SBO),(1T,LBO))

  | Parameters | Value |
  | --- | --- |
  | T | 8 |
  | m | 2 |
  | k | 2 |
  | LBO (relative offset) | 128\*sizeof(bf16) |
  | SBO | 64\*sizeof(bf16) |
  | Encoding of LBO in descriptor | (LBO) >> 4 = 16 |
  | Encoding of SBO in descriptor | (SBO) >> 4 = 8 |
* MN-Major, 32B swizzling and bf16 type: [Figure 191](#tcgen05-mn-32b-swizzle-bf16)

  

[Figure: async-warpgroup-mn-32B-swizzle-bf16]
This figure illustrates the memory layout and swizzling pattern for a tensor memory access scheme with MN major ordering, 32-byte swizzling, and bf16 (bfloat16) data type. It is a 32-row by 16-column grid showing the mapping of logical element indices to physical memory locations (or register indices) after swizzling is applied.

GRID DIMENSIONS:
- Rows: 0 through 31 (32 rows total), labeled on the left vertical axis
- Columns: 0 through 15 (16 columns total), labeled on the top horizontal axis
- Total cells: 512 elements

COLOR CODING:
The cells are color-coded to indicate groupings or phases of the swizzling pattern. The colors repeat in a structured pattern across both rows and columns:
- Green (light green): columns 0-1, rows 0-7 and rows 16-23
- Yellow (light yellow): columns 2-3, rows 0-7 and rows 16-23
- Pink/Red (light pink): columns 4-5, rows 0-7 and rows 16-23
- Blue (light blue): columns 6-7, rows 0-7 and rows 16-23
- Blue (light blue): columns 8-9, rows 0-7 and rows 16-23
- Pink/Red: columns 10-11, rows 0-7 and rows 16-23
- Yellow: columns 12-13, rows 0-7 and rows 16-23
- Green: columns 14-15, rows 0-7 and rows 16-23

The color pattern shifts for rows 8-15 and rows 24-31, implementing the swizzle rotation across row groups.

Row 6:  6, 22, 38, 54, 78, 94, 110, 126, 262, 278, 294, 310, 334, 350, 366, 382

STRUCTURAL PATTERNS AND OBSERVATIONS:

1. The grid is divided into two major horizontal halves: rows 0-15 and rows 16-31. Within rows 0-15, values range from 0 to 383. Within rows 16-31, values range from 128 to 511.

2. Within each group of 8 rows, consecutive rows increment by 1 within each column (e.g., column 0, rows 0-7: 0,1,2,3,4,5,6,7).

3. The column stride within rows 0-7 follows: columns 0-3 increment by 16 (0, 16, 32, 48), columns 4-7 increment by 16 starting at 72 (72, 88, 104, 120), columns 8-11 start at 256 (256, 272, 288, 304), columns 12-15 start at 328 (328, 344, 360, 376).

4. The swizzle pattern is visible in the column groupings: for rows 0-7, columns 4-7 start at 72 (not 64), indicating a swizzle offset of 8 elements. For rows 8-15, columns 4-7 start at 64, showing the swizzle has rotated.

5. The 32-byte swizzle boundary corresponds to 16 bf16 elements (each bf16 is 2 bytes, so 32 bytes = 16 elements). The swizzle rotates the starting column group every 8 rows, which is consistent with a 32-byte swizzling scheme applied to bf16 data.

6. The MN major ordering means the M dimension (rows) is the minor dimension and N


  Figure 191 MN major, 32B swizzling and bf16 type

  the strides and related details are as follows:

  Exact layout : Swizzle<1,4,3> o ((8,2,2),(8,2)):((1,8,128),(16,256))

  Canonical Layout :Swizzle<1,4,3> o ((T,2,m),(8,k)):((1,T,LBO),(2T,SBO))

  | Parameters | Value |
  | --- | --- |
  | T | 8 |
  | m | 2 |
  | k | 2 |
  | LBO (relative offset) | 128\*sizeof(bf16) |
  | SBO | 256\*sizeof(bf16) |
  | Encoding of LBO in descriptor | (LBO) >> 4 = 16 |
  | Encoding of SBO in descriptor | (SBO) >> 4 = 32 |

I'll analyze this figure systematically, extracting all visible data.

CONCEPT BEING ILLUSTRATED:
This figure shows a memory layout/swizzling pattern for matrix data in NVIDIA GPU tensor operations. Specifically, it illustrates MN major ordering with 64-byte swizzling for bf16 (bfloat16) data type. This is used for describing how matrix fragments are laid out in shared memory for tensor core operations.

OVERALL STRUCTURE:
The figure is a large grid/table with:
- Rows numbered 0 through 63 (left side, row index)
- Columns numbered 0 through 15 (top, column index)
- Each cell contains a numerical value
- Cells are color-coded in a repeating pattern

DIMENSIONS:
- 64 rows (0-63)
- 16 columns (0-15)
- Total cells: 64 x 16 = 1024 cells

COLOR CODING PATTERN:
The cells use a repeating color scheme that appears to cycle every 8 rows and follows a pattern across columns. Colors observed include:
- Light blue/cyan
- Light green
- Light orange/peach
- Light purple/lavender
- Light red/pink
- Light yellow
- White/light gray

The color pattern repeats in blocks, suggesting 8-row groupings corresponding to swizzle regions.

  the strides and related details are as follows:

  Exact layout : Swizzle<2,4,3> o ((8,4,2),(8,2)):((1,8,256),(32,512))

  Canonical Layout :Swizzle<2,4,3> o ((T,4,m),(8,k)):((1,T,LBO),(4T,SBO))

  | Parameters | Value |
  | --- | --- |
  | T | 8 |
  | m | 2 |
  | k | 2 |
  | LBO (relative offset) | 256\*sizeof(bf16) |
  | SBO | 512\*sizeof(bf16) |
  | Encoding of LBO in descriptor | (LBO) >> 4 = 32 |
  | Encoding of SBO in descriptor | (SBO) >> 4 = 64 |
